source.definition: a person with whom one works, especially in a profession or business

explanation.sourceText: The English word "colleague" comes from the Latin "collega", meaning a partner or associate in office. It is formed from the prefix "col-" meaning "together" and the verb "legare" meaning "to appoint or delegate."
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „colleague“ provine din latină „collega“, care înseamnă partener sau asociat într-o funcție. Este format din prefixul „col-“, care înseamnă „împreună“, și verbul „legare“, care înseamnă „a desemna sau delega“.

source.additional: The word is commonly used in business and academic contexts.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word "colleg" is directly derived from the Latin "collega", meaning a member of a collegium or a partner at work.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „colleg“ provine din latină „collega“, însemnând „coleg“ sau „partener de muncă“.

target.additional: In Romanian, the word often refers to professional and study colleagues.

explanation.sourceText: Refers to a person who works in the same professional environment.
explanation.targetText: Se referă la o persoană care lucrează în același mediu profesional.

examples.sourceText: I get along well with my colleagues.
examples.targetText: Mă înțeleg bine cu colegii mei.

explanation.sourceText: Refers to someone you study with.
explanation.targetText: Se referă la cineva cu care studiezi.

examples.sourceText: We studied together in the library.
I get along well with my colleagues.
examples.targetText: Mă înțeleg bine cu colegii mei.

examples.sourceText: We study together in the library with our colleagues.
examples.targetText: Învățăm împreună în bibliotecă cu colegii noștri.

examples.sourceText: My colleague comes from another city.
examples.targetText: Colegul meu vine dintr-un alt oraș.

examples.sourceText: We have a new colleague on the team.
examples.targetText: Avem un nou coleg în echipă.

learningTips.text: Remember the English word "colleague" sounds like "colleg" in Romanian, both meaning a work partner.
Think of "colleague" as someone you 'collaborate' with at work or school.
The prefix "col-" means 'together'—so a colleague is someone you work together with.
Imagine your colleague as part of your 'collective' team helping you succeed.
